engineGuide.h2: Toyota Probox Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Toyota Probox across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The 1NZ-FE powered most Probox models from launch, offering proven reliability for commercial use but watch for oil consumption at high mileage. [[51]]
compatibleVariants: NCP58 2WD DX, NCP58 2WD GL, NCP58 2WD GX, NCP59 4WD DX, NCP59 4WD GL, NCP160 2WD, NCP165 4WD
commonFailures: Oil consumption after 100k+ miles due to piston ring wear [[52]]
Rear crankshaft oil seal leaks causing external oil loss [[51]]
Ignition coil failure causing misfire on one cylinder [[54]]
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1NZ-FE Toyota Probox engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: The smaller-displacement 2NZ-FE offered fuel economy for light commercial duty, sharing architecture with the 1NZ-FE but with reduced output.
compatibleVariants: NCP58 2WD DX 1.3, NCP58 2WD GL 1.3, NCP58 2WD GX 1.3
commonFailures: Head gasket failure if coolant maintenance neglected [[49]]
Similar oil consumption issues to 1NZ-FE at high mileage [[61]]
Vacuum leak symptoms around intake manifold gaskets [[63]]
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2NZ-FE Toyota Probox engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Introduced with the 2014 facelift, the 1NR-FE brought Dual VVT-i technology and CVT compatibility but requires careful oil maintenance. [[66]]
compatibleVariants: NCP160 2WD DX CVT, NCP160 2WD GL CVT, NCP160 2WD GX CVT, NSP160 Hybrid base
commonFailures: Excessive oil consumption below 100k km due to piston ring design [[66]]
Timing chain rattle at startup from lack of maintenance [[67]]
Carbon buildup causing piston-to-head contact in some units [[69]]
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1NR-FE Toyota Probox engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Toyota's first aluminium-block diesel, discontinued in 2007, now rare in UK but critical for remaining Probox diesel owners. [[74]]
compatibleVariants: NLP5 2WD DX D-4D, NLP5 2WD GL D-4D, NLP5 2WD GX D-4D

entries.title: pressure fuel pump leaks requiring expensive replacement [[72
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1ND-TV Toyota Probox engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Hybrid Petrol-Electric
entries.history: Latest hybrid variant pairs the proven 1NZ-FE architecture with electric motor assistance for improved fuel economy in commercial use.

entries.title: Hybrid DX, NSP160 Hybrid GL, NSP160 Hybrid GX
commonFailures: Hybrid battery degradation affecting electric assist performance
Inverter cooling system issues at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1NZ-FXE Toyota Probox hybrid engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Toyota Probox Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Toyota Probox Oil Consumption - Why 1NZ-FE & 1NR-FE Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Probox NCP58, NCP59, NCP160, NCP165 (2002-Present, 1NZ-FE & 1NR-FE)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring design allows oil to pass into combustion chamber at high mileage, exacerbated by infrequent oil changes common in commercial use. [[52]]

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Piston Ring Service - Top-end refresh
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: May extend life 20-30k miles if block not worn

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Short Block Replacement - Bottom end rebuild
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace short block assembly with reconditioned unit, reuse head
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution if cylinder bores are worn

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete unit swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt engine with upgraded rings, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage commercial Probox, includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Probox worth £3,000-£6,000, if oil consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles and compression is low, a full rebuilt engine offers better long-term value than repeated top-end repairs. We recommend getting multiple quotes before committing.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1NZ-FE engine prices for your Toyota Probox

problems.h4: Toyota Probox Timing Chain Rattle - Cold Start Noise on 1NR-FE Engines
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear combined with oil consumption reduces lubrication pressure, causing chain slap at cold startup. [[67]]

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Tensioner Replacement - Early intervention
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain tensioner and guide rails, retain existing chain
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before chain stretch damages components

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Updated Timing Components - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1NR-FE with upgraded tensioner, chain, and oil control rings
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for Probox with rattle plus oil consumption - addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: If you hear chain rattle only on cold start and oil consumption is normal, a tensioner replacement may suffice. But if rattle persists when warm or oil consumption is high, replacement with a rebuilt engine featuring updated components is the reliable commercial choice.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1NR-FE Toyota Probox timing component replacement

problems.h4: Toyota Probox 1ND-TV Injector Failure - Diesel Rough Running & Loss of Power
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common rail injector seals degrade, allowing fuel leakage into cylinders causing misfire, black smoke, and potential hydro-lock risk. [[75]]

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Injector Seal Replacement - Address leakage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace seals and washers, recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if injectors themselves are still functional

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Injector Replacement (Set of 4) - Full injector refresh
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four common rail injectors with reconditioned units, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block and turbo are healthy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 1ND-TV Engine - Complete diesel unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1ND-TV with new injectors, turbo inspection, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for high-mileage diesel Probox where multiple systems show wear

problems.recommendation: Given the rarity of the 1ND-TV in the UK and the cost of quality injectors, if your Probox diesel has exceeded 150k miles and shows injector symptoms alongside turbo or EGR issues, a complete rebuilt engine often represents better value than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1ND-TV diesel engine prices for your Toyota Probox

problems.h4: Toyota Probox Head Gasket Failure - Coolant Neglect on 2NZ-FE Engines
problems.rootCause: Infrequent coolant changes allow corrosion and scale buildup, weakening head gasket seal between cylinders and coolant passages. [[49]]

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Head Gasket Replacement - Top-end repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine head surface if warped
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is not cracked and valves are undamaged

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 2NZ-FE Engine - Complete unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2NZ-FE with updated gasket materials, pressure tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating caused warping or if Probox has high commercial mileage

problems.recommendation: If your 1.3L Probox has overheated but the block is not cracked, a head gasket replacement by a specialist can restore reliability. However, for commercial Probox vans used for high-mileage delivery work, investing in a complete rebuilt engine with warranty often provides better downtime protection.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt 2NZ-FE Toyota Probox engine options UK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Toyota Probox
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Probox suffers from oil consumption, timing noise, injector issues or head gasket failure, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand JDM commercial vans. Compare rebuilt, reconditioned and used engine prices from vetted suppliers, all backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Toyota Probox Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Toyota Probox and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Toyota Probox Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDescription: Professionally rebuilt to OEM spec with upgraded wear components, bench-tested and ready for direct fitment to your Probox.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code, variant and supplier location.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Probox engines undergo full strip, clean, measurement and replacement of worn parts like rings, bearings and seals. [[49]]
backBullets: Includes upgraded piston rings to address oil consumption common on NZ engines

Minimum 12-month warranty from UK Toyota Probox engine specialists

types.title: Toyota Probox Rebuilt Engines

commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on 2NZ-FE if coolant neglected; oil consumption

items.title: Toyota Probox 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5L 1NZ-FE is the backbone of the Probox range, fitted to most 2WD and 4WD variants from 2002 onward, valued for proven reliability in commercial use despite oil consumption at high mileage. [[7]]

items.title: Toyota Probox 1.4L Diesel Engines

fuelTypes.h2: Toyota Probox Engines by Fuel Type

items.title: Toyota Probox Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines dominate the Probox range, with the proven 1NZ-FE 1.5L and updated 1NR-FE 1.3L offering reliability for commercial use, though oil consumption at high mileage is a known consideration. [[7]]
items.descriptor: Petrol engines dominate the Probox range, with the proven 1NZ-FE 1.5L and updated 1NR-FE 1.3L offering reliability for commercial use, though oil consumption at high mileage is a known consideration. [[7]]
items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota Probox Petrol Engine Families
families: 1NZ-FE - Most common replacement engine (NCP58/NCP59), watch for oil consumption after 100k miles [[52]]
2NZ-FE - Pre-2014 1.3L unit, shares architecture with 1NZ-FE, head gasket risk if coolant neglected [[49]]
1NR-FE - Post-2014 1.3L with Dual VVT-i, requires careful oil maintenance to avoid timing issues [[66]]
knownFor: Strong reliability record for commercial high-mileage use when maintained
Oil consumption on NZ-family engines after 100k+ miles is common [[51]]
Cross-model compatibility: engines shared with Yaris, Platz, Porte, Corolla [[9]]
2014 facelift introduced incompatible 1NR-FE/CVT combination requiring careful sourcing
items.cta: Compare Toyota Probox Petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Probox Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L 1ND-TV diesel was offered only until 2007, making it rare in the UK but valued for torque in commercial applications where it remains in service. [[74]]
items.descriptor: The 1.4L 1ND-TV diesel was offered only until 2007, making it rare in the UK but valued for torque in commercial applications where it remains in service. [[74]]
items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota Probox Diesel Engine Families
families: 1ND-TV - Only diesel option, discontinued 2007, scarce UK availability but high owner intent [[72]]
knownFor: Strong low-end torque suitable for loaded commercial use
High-pressure fuel pump and injector seal failures common at high mileage [[75]]
Very limited UK supply; most replacements sourced from Japanese donors
Owners report excellent fuel economy when maintained with quality diesel
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Probox Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Toyota Probox Hybrid Engines
items.description: The hybrid Probox launched in 2026 pairs petrol and electric power for improved urban efficiency, representing a niche but growing segment for commercial operators. [[9]]
items.descriptor: The hybrid Probox launched in 2026 pairs petrol and electric power for improved urban efficiency, representing a niche but growing segment for commercial operators. [[9]]
families: 1NZ-FXE - Hybrid variant of the proven 1NZ-FE, paired with electric motor and battery pack
Petrol engine provides primary power with electric motor assisting acceleration and low-speed driving
Regenerative braking recharges battery; no plug-in capability for commercial simplicity
Replacement requires specialist assessment of both combustion and electric components
importantNotes: Hybrid Probox is new to UK grey import market; replacement parts availability still developing
Engine replacement may require hybrid system inspection and battery health verification
Specialist knowledge essential; not all UK engine suppliers handle hybrid variants

engineYears.h2: Toyota Probox Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Toyota Probox production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: NZ Family & Rare Diesel
years.description: The Probox launched with three engine options: 1.3L and 1.5L petrol plus a rare 1.4L diesel, establishing the chassis codes still referenced by UK importers today. [[46]]
keyChanges: Toyota Probox introduced to Japanese market as commercial light van/wagon
Chassis codes NCP58 (2WD) and NCP59 (4WD) established
Diesel variant discontinued in September 2007, making later replacements scarce
knownFor: NZ-family petrol engines praised for commercial durability when maintained
1ND-TV diesel valued for torque but scarce parts in UK market
Early models lack modern emissions equipment, simplifying some repairs
Cross-model compatibility with Yaris, Platz, Porte aids UK sourcing [[9]]

years.preview: Mature NZ Generation: Refinement & High Mileage
years.description: Mid-life Probox models continued with proven NZ-family engines, with many UK-imported examples now reaching high commercial mileage requiring replacement.
keyChanges: Minor facelifts updated styling but retained core NZ-family engine lineup
No major mechanical changes; focus on commercial reliability and cost
UK grey import market grew for these robust, simple commercial vans
knownFor: High-mileage examples common in UK commercial use; oil consumption emerges as key issue [[52]]
Simple mechanical design aids specialist repair and rebuild in UK
4WD NCP59 variants require specific engine with different sump/mounts
Strong donor compatibility with UK-sourced Yaris, Platz, Corolla 1NZ-FE units

years.ticker: Toyota Probox NCP59 4WD engine • Toyota Probox 1NZ-FE rebuilt price • Toyota Probox oil consumption fix

years.preview: Facelift Generation: NR Family & CVT Introduction
years.description: The 2014 facelift introduced the 1NR-FE engine and CVT transmission for 1.3L models, creating a critical compatibility boundary for UK replacement sourcing. [[20]]
keyChanges: 1.3L engine upgraded from 2NZ-FE to 1NR-FE with Dual VVT-i technology
4-speed automatic replaced by CVT on 1.3L variants; engines not interchangeable with pre-2014
1.5L 1NZ-FE continued with minor updates for NCP160/NCP165 chassis
Improved fuel economy and emissions for commercial fleet appeal
knownFor: 1NR-FE requires careful oil maintenance to avoid timing chain rattle at startup [[67]]
CVT compatibility means pre-2014 engines cannot be swapped without major modification
Cross-model compatibility continues with newer Yaris, Vitz 1NR-FE donors
Commercial operators value improved fuel economy of facelift models

years.ticker: Toyota Probox 1NR-FE CVT engine • Toyota Probox 2014 facelift compatibility • Toyota Probox timing chain replacement

years.preview: Present - Hybrid Introduction: Electrified Commercial Option
years.description: The latest Probox introduces hybrid powertrains for improved urban efficiency, representing a new challenge for UK replacement sourcing and specialist knowledge. [[9]]
keyChanges: Hybrid variant launched with 1NZ-FXE petrol-electric powertrain
Improved fuel economy targets urban commercial delivery applications
New chassis code NSP160 for hybrid models; compatibility with petrol variants limited
UK grey import market beginning to receive these latest models
knownFor: Hybrid system adds complexity; replacement requires specialist assessment of battery and inverter
Early UK availability means limited donor stock; planning ahead essential
Commercial operators value reduced fuel costs in stop-start urban delivery use
Minimum 12-month warranty coverage essential for hybrid component protection

faq.h2: Toyota Probox Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Toyota Probox engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Toyota Probox engines typically cost £1,100-£3,200 supply-only depending on engine code, with 1NZ-FE 1.5L petrol at the lower end and rare 1ND-TV diesel or hybrid variants at the premium. [[78]]
keyPoints: 1NZ-FE 1.5L petrol rebuilt: £1,200-£2,200 supply-only
1NR-FE 1.3L petrol CVT rebuilt: £1,300-£2,400 supply-only
Rare 1ND-TV 1.4L diesel rebuilt: £1,800-£3,200 due to scarcity
Supply & fit service adds £400-£800 labour depending on location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Toyota Probox engine prices for your variant

items.question: Will a Toyota Yaris engine fit my Toyota Probox?
items.answer: Yes, many Toyota Probox engines are shared with Yaris, Platz, Porte and Corolla models, making UK sourcing easier through cross-model donor compatibility. [[9]]
keyPoints: 1NZ-FE 1.5L fitted to Yaris, Platz, Porte, Corolla, Probox
2NZ-FE 1.3L shared across same model range pre-2014
Always verify engine code and chassis compatibility before purchase
DVLA registration check confirms exact engine code for your Probox
items.cta: Check compatible Toyota Yaris engine options for your Probox

items.question: What is the difference between Toyota Probox NCP58 and NCP59?
items.answer: NCP58 denotes 2WD front-wheel drive Probox models, while NCP59 indicates 4WD full-time V-flex system variants with different engine mounting and sump configuration. [[11]]
keyPoints: NCP58: 2WD, standard engine configuration, most common in UK imports
NCP59: 4WD, different sump, mounts, and drivetrain components
Engines are not directly interchangeable between 2WD and 4WD variants
Always specify chassis code when requesting quotes to ensure correct fitment
items.cta: Get quotes for your Toyota Probox NCP58 or NCP59 engine

items.question: Are Toyota Probox engines reliable for commercial use?
items.answer: Toyota Probox NZ-family engines have strong reliability records for commercial high-mileage use when maintained, though oil consumption emerges after 100k miles on many examples. [[7]]
keyPoints: 1NZ-FE and 2NZ-FE proven in commercial service across multiple Toyota models
Regular oil changes critical to minimise consumption and extend life
Simple mechanical design aids specialist repair and rebuild in UK
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components offer best value for high-mileage vans
items.cta: Find reliable rebuilt Toyota Probox engines for commercial use

items.question: How do I find the engine code for my Toyota Probox?
items.answer: Your Toyota Probox engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, visible on the VIN plate under the bonnet, or confirmed instantly via DVLA registration check. [[41]]
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block: 1NZ-FE, 2NZ-FE, 1NR-FE or 1ND-TV
VIN plate under bonnet lists engine code for verification
DVLA registration lookup confirms exact engine for your chassis
Chassis code (NCP58/59/160) also essential for correct replacement sourcing
items.cta: Identify your Toyota Probox engine code with our free check

items.question: Does a used Toyota Probox engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Warranty terms vary by supplier, but engines sourced through EngineMarket's network include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt and reconditioned units. [[79]]
keyPoints: Used engines may have limited or no warranty; always request terms
Rebuilt/reconditioned engines typically include 12-month minimum warranty
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects, not misuse
Confirm warranty provider and claim process before completing purchase
items.cta: Compare Toyota Probox engine warranty options from UK suppliers

items.question: How long does it take to fit a Toyota Probox engine?
items.answer: Professional fitting of a Toyota Probox engine typically takes 1-2 days depending on variant complexity, with 4WD and CVT models requiring additional time for drivetrain alignment.
keyPoints: Standard 2WD petrol Probox: 1 day for experienced specialist
4WD or CVT variants: 1.5-2 days due to additional components
Supply & fit service includes removal, fitting, fluids and testing
Commercial operators should book in advance to minimise vehicle downtime
items.cta: Find Toyota Probox engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Toyota Probox?
items.answer: For commercial Toyota Probox vans valued at £3,000-£6,000, engine replacement with a rebuilt unit often represents better value than vehicle replacement, especially with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost £1,100-£3,200 vs Probox market value £3k-£6k
Warranty coverage protects investment and minimises future downtime
Simple mechanical design keeps labour costs lower than complex modern vans
Cross-model donor compatibility improves parts availability and pricing
items.cta: Calculate Toyota Probox repair vs replace value with our tool

items.question: What is the difference between 2NZ-FE and 1NR-FE engines?
items.answer: The 2NZ-FE is the pre-2014 1.3L petrol engine, while the 1NR-FE is the post-2014 facelift unit with Dual VVT-i and CVT compatibility; they are not interchangeable without major modification. [[20]]
keyPoints: 2NZ-FE: 86-88hp, 4-speed auto compatible, pre-2014 NCP58 models
1NR-FE: 92-95hp, Dual VVT-i, CVT only, post-2014 NCP160 models
Different engine mounts, ECU, and transmission interfaces
Always verify production year and chassis code before ordering replacement
items.cta: Compare 2NZ-FE vs 1NR-FE Toyota Probox engine options

items.question: Can I convert my Toyota Probox to the hybrid engine?
items.answer: Converting a non-hybrid Toyota Probox to hybrid powertrain is not recommended due to complex integration of battery, inverter, ECU and drivetrain components requiring factory-level engineering.
keyPoints: Hybrid Probox uses unique NSP160 chassis with integrated electrical architecture
Conversion would require extensive modification beyond cost-effectiveness
UK vehicle approval (IVA) would be challenging for such a conversion
Better value to source correct engine code for your existing chassis
items.cta: Enquire about correct Toyota Probox hybrid engine for your chassis
